articles.title: Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract
articles.description:
Gonzalez is looking for an extension that would pay him top NFL corner money.

The post Report: Christian Gonzalez unlikely to participate in OTAs under current Patriots contract appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Christian Gonzalez may miss OTAs due to contract disputes with the Patriots.
Gonzalez seeks a contract extension for top NFL cornerback salary.
His absence could affect team dynamics and preparation for the upcoming season.

articles.title: Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham
articles.description:
No injuries were reported after the vehicle struck a State Police cruiser and nearly hit a Malden police officer, authorities said.

The post Search underway for driver who led cops on a chase through Everett, Malden, Stoneham appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Police are searching for a driver who led a chase through Everett, Malden, and Stoneham.
The suspect's vehicle struck a State Police cruiser without causing injuries on May 29.
Malden police narrowly avoided injury as the vehicle nearly hit an officer during the pursuit.

articles.title: Federal judge says NH must make it easier to prove citizenship when registering to vote
articles.description:
The judge noted that an expert on voter fraud found only 47 instances of wrongful voting out of roughly 8.3 million votes between 1998 and 2024. During that time, only eight noncitizens may have cast ballots, she said.

The post Federal judge says NH must make it easier to prove citizenship when registering to vote appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Federal judge mandates NH simplify citizenship proof for voter registration.
Expert found only 47 wrongful votes among 8.3 million from 1998 to 2024.
Changes may influence voter registration processes affecting Boston residents.

articles.title: Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football
articles.description:
Mayo joined Fifth Down Capital as the company’s managing director in February.

The post Ex-Patriots HC Jerod Mayo has a new job away from football appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jerod Mayo joined Fifth Down Capital as managing director in February 2023.
Mayo's transition from football to finance may inspire local athletes pursuing diverse careers.
Fifth Down Capital's presence in Boston could enhance local investment opportunities.
